{"step_by_step_thinking": "The patient is a 76-year-old female with a 2x3 cm non-healing ulcer on the labia majora, which is consistent with squamous cell carcinoma. The biopsy confirms the diagnosis. Given the size of the tumor (greater than 2 cm), the standard treatment is wide local excision with a margin of 1-2 cm. However, since the tumor is larger than 2 cm, radical vulvectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy is recommended to ensure adequate margins and assess for lymph node involvement. Chemoradiation with resection is typically reserved for locally advanced disease or when the patient is not a candidate for surgery.", "answer_choice": "A"}
